# Wire — Pipe — conn-or-pool dispatch

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> [`test/stories/wire_pipe_dispatch_story_test.exs`](../../../../echo/apps/echo_mq/test/stories/wire_pipe_dispatch_story_test.exs). Every scenario below is a passing
> ExUnit test driving the real `EchoMQ` surface against Valkey, so the catalogue cannot
> drift from the code.

**2 scenarios.**

## Scenario: the same %Pipe{} flushes identically against a Connector and a Pool

- **Given** a pipe built once over the connector (set, get, incr a sibling counter)
- **When** it is flushed against the connector, then the same built batch is re-targeted to the pool
- **Then** both round-trips return the identical reply list

## Scenario: exec_txn wraps the batch in MULTI/EXEC against a single Connector

- **Given** a counter key
- **When** two increments are flushed as one transaction
- **Then** EXEC returns both step replies in order
- **And** exec_noreply suppresses replies wire-side and answers :ok
